bash_commands.command: find kubernetes--v1.32.0 -type f -name "*.go" | xargs grep -l "Immutable" | head -30
find kubernetes--v1.32.0/pkg/registry -type f -name "*.go" | xargs grep -l "ConfigMap\|Secret" | head -20
find kubernetes--v1.32.0 -name "*test.go" -type f | xargs grep -l "Immutable" | head -20
find kubernetes--v1.32.0 -type f -name "*.go" -path "*/configmap/*" -o -path "*/secret/*" | grep -E "(configmap|secret)" | grep -v vendor | head -30

  ],
  "text": "The ConfigMap and Secret immutability feature in kubernetes/kubernetes is implemented across multiple layers:\n\n## API Type Definitions\nThe Immutable field is defined in both the v1 API and internal API types:\n- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go` contains the public v1 API definitions for Secret (line 7385) and ConfigMap (line 7528) with an `Immutable *bool` field\n- `pkg/apis/core/types.go` contains the internal API definitions for Secret (line 6145) and ConfigMap (line 6273)\n\n## Validation and Mutation Prevention Logic\nThe `pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go` file implements the core immutability validation:\n- `ValidateSecret` (line 6764): Basic validation for secret structure\n- `ValidateSecretUpdate` (line 6841): Checks that when a secret has `Immutable=true`, neither the `immutable` field nor the `data` field can be modified\n- `ValidateConfigMap` (line 6866): Basic validation for ConfigMap structure\n- `ValidateConfigMapUpdate` (line 6898): Checks that when a ConfigMap has `Immutable=true`, neither the `immutable` field, `data`, nor `binaryData` fields can be modified\n- `ValidateImmutableField` (line 363): Helper function for immutable field validation\n\nThe strategy layer in `pkg/registry/core/configmap/strategy.go` and `pkg/registry/core/secret/strategy.go` delegates to these validation functions in the `ValidateUpdate` methods, enforcing mutation prevention at the REST API level.\n\n## Watch Optimization for Immutable Objects\nThe `pkg/kubelet/util/manager/watch_based_manager.go` implements an optimization that skips watching immutable objects:\n- Type `isImmutableFunc` (line 44): Function type that determines if an object is immutable\n- `objectCacheItem.setImmutable()` (line 90): Marks an object cache item as immutable\n- `objectCache.Get()` (line 304-348): After retrieving an object, if it's immutable, the method calls `item.setImmutable()` and `item.stop()` to stop the reflector, preventing unnecessary watches on objects that will never change\n\n## Manager Implementations\nThe kubelet managers provide the `isImmutable` predicate functions:\n- `pkg/kubelet/configmap/configmap_manager.go` - `NewWatchingConfigMapManager` (line 138) creates a ConfigMap manager with an `isImmutable` function (lines 148-153) that checks if `configMap.Immutable != nil && *configMap.Immutable`\n- `pkg/kubelet/secret/secret_manager.go` - `NewWatchingSecretManager` (line 139) creates a Secret manager with an `isImmutable` function (lines 149-154) that checks if `secret.Immutable != nil && *secret.Immutable`\n\n## Storage and REST API Layer\nThe storage implementations in `pkg/registry/core/configmap/storage/storage.go` and `pkg/registry/core/secret/storage/storage.go` use the strategy objects which enforce immutability validation.\n\n## Testing\nThe implementation is tested in:\n- `pkg/apis/core/validation/validation_test.go`: Comprehensive validation tests for ConfigMap and Secret immutability constraints\n- `pkg/kubelet/util/manager/watch_based_manager_test.go`: `TestImmutableSecretStopsTheReflector` (line 210) verifies that immutable secrets stop the reflector\n- `test/integration/kubelet/watch_manager_test.go`: Integration tests for watch manager behavior\n\nThe feature provides both API-level mutation prevention (preventing changes to immutable ConfigMaps and Secrets via the Kubernetes API) and kubelet-level optimization (stopping watches for immutable objects to reduce resource usage)."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Kubernetes ConfigMap Immutability Feature Migration

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in kubernetes/kubernetes that implement the ConfigMap and Secret immutability feature: the Immutable field validation, the mutation prevention logic, and the watch optimization that skips immutable objects.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the migration domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.32.0, sg-evals/client-go--v0.32.0, sg-evals/api--v0.32.0, sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/etcd-io-etcd` (etcd-io/etcd)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?

I'll help you find all Go source files implementing the ConfigMap and Secret immutability feature in kubernetes/kubernetes. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and searching for relevant files.
Now let me create a comprehensive summary of all the files I've found related to the ConfigMap and Secret immutability feature.
Let me verify my findings by checking if there are any other important files I may have missed:

Perfect! I have successfully identified and documented all Go source files implementing the ConfigMap and Secret immutability feature in kubernetes/kubernetes.

## Summary

I've found **13 key source files** and **17 important symbols** that implement the ConfigMap and Secret immutability feature across three main aspects:

### **1. Immutable Field Definitions**
- **v1 API types** in `staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/types.go` (Secret at line 7385, ConfigMap at line 7528)
- **Internal API types** in `pkg/apis/core/types.go`

### **2. Field Validation & Mutation Prevention**
- **Core validation logic** in `pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go`:
  - `ValidateSecret` and `ValidateSecretUpdate` (lines 6764, 6841)
  - `ValidateConfigMap` and `ValidateConfigMapUpdate` (lines 6866, 6898)
  - These functions prevent mutations when `Immutable=true`
  
- **Strategy layer** in `pkg/registry/core/configmap/strategy.go` and `pkg/registry/core/secret/strategy.go` enforces validation at the REST API level

### **3. Watch Optimization**
- **Watch-based manager** in `pkg/kubelet/util/manager/watch_based_manager.go`:
  - Uses `isImmutableFunc` type and `objectCacheItem.setImmutable()` method
  - The `objectCache.Get()` method (lines 304-348) stops watching immutable objects to reduce resource usage
  
- **Manager implementations** in `pkg/kubelet/configmap/configmap_manager.go` and `pkg/kubelet/secret/secret_manager.go` provide the immutability check functions

The complete analysis has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with detailed documentation of files, symbols, implementation chains, and a comprehensive narrative explaining how the feature works across the API, validation, storage, and kubelet layers.

  "text": "The ConfigMap and Secret immutability feature in kubernetes/kubernetes is implemented across multiple layers:\n\n## API Type Definitions\nThe Immutable field is defined in both the v1 API `pkg/apis/core/types.go` contains the internal API definitions for Secret (line 6145) and ConfigMap (line 6273)\n\n## Validation and Mutation Prevention Logic\nThe `pkg/apis/core/validation/validation.go` file implements the core immutability validation:\n- `ValidateSecret` (line 6764): Basic validation for secret structure\n- `ValidateSecretUpdate` (line 6841): Checks that when a secret has `Immutable=true`, neither the `immutable` field nor the `data` field can be modified\n- `ValidateConfigMap` (line 6866): Basic validation for ConfigMap structure\n- functions in the `ValidateUpdate` methods, enforcing mutation prevention at the REST API level.\n\n## Watch Optimization for Immutable Objects\nThe `pkg/kubelet/util/manager/watch_based_manager.go` 90): Marks an object cache item as immutable\n- `objectCache.Get()` (line 304-348): After retrieving an object, if it's immutable, the method calls `item.setImmutable()` and `item.stop()` to stop the reflector, preventing unnecessary watches on objects that will never change\n\n## Manager Implementations\nThe kubelet managers provide the `isImmutable` predicate functions:\n- `pkg/kubelet/configmap/configmap_manager.go` - `NewWatchingConfigMapManager` (line 138) creates a ConfigMap manager with an `isImmutable` function (lines 148-153) that checks if `configMap.Immutable != nil && *configMap.Immutable`\n- `pkg/kubelet/secret/secret_manager.go` - `NewWatchingSecretManager` (line 139) creates a Secret manager with an `isImmutable` function (lines 149-154) that checks if `secret.Immutable != nil && *secret.Immutable`\n\n## Storage and REST API Layer\nThe storage implementations in `pkg/registry/core/configmap/storage/storage.go` and `pkg/registry/core/secret/storage/storage.go` use the strategy objects which enforce immutability validation.\n\n## Testing\nThe implementation is tested in:\n- `TestImmutableSecretStopsTheReflector` (line 210) verifies that immutable secrets stop the reflector\n- `test/integration/kubelet/watch_manager_test.go`: Integration tests for watch manager immutable objects to reduce resource usage)."
